x-artifact.note: Design-first artifact only — describes the MCP tool surface, not a running server. Tool input/output schemas $ref the same object schemas as the OpenAPI; each tool maps to an OpenAPI operationId. Surfaces NYCHA's open reference data (developments, addresses, facilities, utility metering, resident statistics) and adds report_repair — the net-new write workflow locked today inside the Oracle Siebel Self Service Portal.

server.name: io.nyc.nycha
server.title: NYC Housing Authority (NYCHA)
server.description: Agent-native access to the New York City Housing Authority — public housing developments, residential addresses, community facilities, utility consumption metering, and aggregate resident statistics from NYC Open Data, plus the net-new ability to report and track a maintenance repair (work order) that today lives only in the Siebel Self Service Portal.
server.instructions: Use find_developments / get_development (and get_development_addresses) to locate public housing developments by borough, program, or council district and drill into their buildings. find_facilities covers community facilities, senior/day-care centers, and customer contact centers. find_utility_consumption answers energy/water cost-and-consumption questions per development (PropertyMeter). find_resident_statistics returns AGGREGATE demographics only — NYCHA never exposes individual households. report_repair (net-new) creates a maintenance work order; it requires an authenticated resident and mutates state — confirm the unit, category, and problem with the user before submitting, and set emergency=true for no-heat/hot-water/gas/flooding so it routes to the 24-hour contact center.

tools.name: find_developments
tools.title: Find Developments
tools.description: Search NYCHA public housing developments by borough, program, senior status, council district, and/or free text.

tools.name: get_development
tools.title: Get Development
tools.description: Fetch a NYCHA development (units, buildings, population, geography) by TDS number.

tools.name: get_development_addresses
tools.title: Get Development Addresses
tools.description: List the residential building addresses within a development.

tools.name: find_addresses
tools.title: Find Addresses
tools.description: Search NYCHA residential addresses by borough, BIN, BBL, ZIP, or free text.

tools.name: find_facilities
tools.title: Find Facilities
tools.description: List NYCHA community facilities, service centers, and customer contact centers by borough, type, program type, or development.

tools.name: find_utility_consumption
tools.title: Find Utility Consumption
tools.description: Search development-level utility consumption and cost metering (electric, water, gas, steam, oil) — the PropertyMeter data — by utility, development, borough, or period.

tools.name: find_resident_statistics
tools.title: Find Resident Statistics
tools.description: Return AGGREGATE resident demographics by development or program (population, families, income, age bands). Never returns individual households.

tools.name: list_my_work_orders
tools.title: List My Work Orders
tools.description: List the authenticated resident's own repair work orders and their status — today visible only inside the Siebel Self Service Portal.

tools.name: report_repair
tools.title: Report a Repair
tools.description: Create a NYCHA maintenance work order (report a repair). NET-NEW workflow with no public API today — it lives only in the Oracle Siebel Self Service Portal or a phone call to the Customer Contact Center. Requires an authenticated resident. Mutates state — confirm unit, category, and problem first; set emergency=true for no heat/hot water, gas, or flooding.
